title: Update: Memorial Service for Chairman Henry Ivanoff
summary: Update: A Memorial Service for Chairman Ivanoff will take place in Anchorage at 3 p.m. on Sunday, April 3 at the First Evangelical Covenant Church (1145 C. Street). A Celebration of Life will take place in Unalakleet on April 9 at 2 p.m.

title: One new hospitalization and thirteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta April 1
summary: On April 1, YKHC reports one new in-region hospitalization and thirteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. One In-Region Hospitalization YKHC reports that one COVID-19 positive individual was admitted into the Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta Regional Hospital. This is the 149th individual hospitalized in-...

title: Good¢ents Grants Awarded in February 2022
summary: The Good¢ents Board of Trustees met on February 16, 2022 and awarded 6 grants totaling $21,150.

title: Lies and Fortune Cookies (Happy April 1)
summary: The dubious backstories behind a few Alaskan artists and the fortunes of the others as told through cookies. Or as we call it, April 1.

title: Unlocking the Genes of Wood Frogs
summary: Studying wood frogs requires first finding the tiny animals in the vast Arctic, then preserving their DNA while huddled in a tent. The post Unlocking the Genes of Wood Frogs appeared first on Alaska Magazine .

title: Naomi Hutchquist is 2022 Spirit of KUAC Recipient
summary: Naomi Hutchquist uses her professional skills along with a dose of her fun side to volunteer for KUAC. For several years she has created KUAC’s graphic design work, from mugs to masks, annual reporting projects to postcards. “KUAC is a good cause and I like helping out,” she said. “I like the people...

title: Upcoming changes to COVID-19 testing hours in Bethel
summary: In response to the recent drop in new COVID-19 infections, effective Monday, April 4, COVID-19 testing hours at YKHC will change. Between Monday, April 4, and Friday, April 15 , PCR and Binax tests will be available at the outdoor testing site across the street from the hospital between 10 a.m. and...
